mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly elevating equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an approaching economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that depresses S&P 500 prices over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth outpaces housing starts, supply chain bottlenecks emerge, compressing corporate margins and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression, or forward returns are not significantly lower when INDPRO growth exceeds HOUST growth by 2 standard deviations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, elevating the cost of capital and suppressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally persistently despite sustained inflation acceleration, or the inflation signal fails to predict negative forward returns in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the historical trend rate of 2.5 percent signals robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when GDP growth exceeds trend, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden surge in nonfarm payrolls indicates overheating labor markets, which historically precedes Fed tightening and subsequent equity drawdowns over the following month.
failure_condition: Markets continue to rally for 20 days following large payroll beats, or the signal exhibits no statistical edge in predicting negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: When residential construction permits fall while short-term interest rates remain elevated, credit conditions tighten simultaneously, choking off demand and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both conditions hold, or forward returns are positive in >55% of co-occurring instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial output signals an immediate supply-side shock or demand collapse, triggering algorithmic risk reduction and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally immediately following sharp industrial output declines, or the signal's predictive power vanishes in out-of-sample testing.
